title: Making Friends at the Gym: A Fun Way to Stay Healthy
summary: This article talks about how young people are making friends at the gym instead of going to bars or clubs. Sydney Cassidy, a 28-year-old, says she meets her friends at the gym and doesn't need to drink to have fun. After Covid, more people are seeing gyms as social places where they can hang out and feel part of a community. Gyms are playing music and having fun classes that make it feel like a party. Many young people are joining gyms and making new friends there. They think wellness means more than just exercise—it's also about being happy with friends. Gyms are becoming popular spots for socializing, like pubs used to be.

people you like to spend time with

being healthy and happy in body and mind
a group of people who share interests and support each other

questions.question: What are gyms becoming like, according to the article?

background_read: This article is about how young adults, especially in their twenties, are changing how they socialize. Instead of going to bars or clubs where people often drink alcohol, they are going to gyms to exercise and make friends. This trend started growing after the Covid-19 pandemic, as people looked for safe and healthy ways to connect. Gyms are now offering fun classes with music and lights to attract more young members. It shows that being healthy isn't just about working out—it's also about feeling happy and connected with others in a community.
Article_Structure: WHO: Young people like Sydney Cassidy, a 28-year-old digital creator, and other twenty-somethings who go to gyms.
WHAT: They are using gyms as places to meet friends and socialize without drinking, treating them like community hangouts.
WHERE: This happens in gyms such as PureGym and Oasis Health Club in places like the UK and Grimsby.
WHY: Because they want a healthy social life, avoid drinking culture, and see wellness as more than just exercise, leading gyms to become alternative social spaces.

perspectives.description: Young people are choosing gyms over bars for socializing, showing a move towards healthier lifestyles.
